campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to help people get around by walking, biking, public transit, and cars.
keyTopics: City investment performance and financial transactions for July 2025
Monthly financial health and treasurer's report
New multimodal transportation plan to improve walking, biking, and transit options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, they introduced an ordinance (a new city rule) and decided to skip the standard first reading process to move things along faster. The council directed staff to put this ordinance on the consent calendar so it can be officially adopted at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance was waived to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Staff directed to add ordinance to consent calendar

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from multiple sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The council reviewed records from regular meetings, special sessions, and closed-door discussions to ensure accuracy.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March 2026 meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Regular administrative and record-keeping tasks

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving its young people through various programs and services. They discussed youth programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and youth intervention services, looked at how city code enforcement is working, and reviewed plans to improve library facilities and services for residents.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer programs for kids
Code Enforcement annual performance review
Library facility improvements and customer experience
Youth intervention services progress report

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and virtual attendance options. Residents were able to join online via Zoom or phone to listen and give public comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Meeting offered hybrid format with Zoom and phone dial-in options
Public comment period available for residents during agenda items
Virtual participants could raise hands or press *9 to request speaking time

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission met on April 9, 2026, to hear personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in the community. The group also approved the meeting minutes from their previous gathering in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The council heard presentations about a food program called Budget and Bites, a youth organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous session in March.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites food program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer jobs program for youth
Public comment period for residents
Approval of previous meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using a hybrid format so residents could attend either in person or online. The council is making it easy for the public to participate and share their opinions by offering multiple ways to join and submit comments.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options via Zoom video conference
Phone dial-in access for those without video
eComment system for submitting written public comments before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal housing and community development plan for the next five years, new project requests from individual council members, and a proposed change to how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests from council members
Changes to the process for filling council member vacancies
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's natural gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The council reviewed and approved changes to residential and commercial gas service rates, along with how utility reserves and funds will be managed.
keyTopics: Natural gas utility budget approval for 2027
Gas rate changes for residential customers (G-1 rate schedule)
Gas rate changes for commercial customers (G-2 and G-3 rate schedules)
Transfer of utility reserves and general fund allocations
